name: Belton Union Bus Ctr Inc
recent_news: No web presence located; the listed address (429 Sidney Baker St, Kerrville TX) is the Kerrville Police Department, suggesting the record may be stale, mis-coded, or a defunct legacy listing. Name 'Belton Union Bus Center' does not appear in current TX school-bus contractor results or DMV/USDOT directories (2026).
fit_notes: Likely not an active, identifiable school-bus operator at this address; appears to be a data-quality artifact. Not a viable Summit tuck-in target without further validation (state SOS / TxDPS bus operator lookup).

name: Freedom 2 Go Sch Trnsprtn Inc
districts_or_customers: private school contracts available upon request (limited)
Northeast Ohio NEMT and airport clientele
recent_news: No M&A or notable news found (2024-2026). Company markets itself primarily as a Cleveland-area black car/airport/NEMT operator that also offers private school transportation 'upon request' with limited capacity.
fit_notes: Tiny mixed-services operator (~5 vehicles: sedans, Sprinters, vans) — not a yellow-bus contractor with district routes. Low strategic fit for Summit; school transport is an ancillary line, no scaled district book of business.

name: Dietrich's Of Mitchell Inc
owner_or_parent: Dietrich & Sons Inc / Dietrich/Thomas Bus Sales (Valley City, ND); Richard Dietrich family
districts_or_customers: Lost Mitchell School District 17-2 (SD) contract to Foreman Bus Company effective 2017 after 15 years of service
recent_news: April 2017: Mitchell SD Board of Education awarded 7-route district contract to Foreman Bus Company over Dietrich's after a ~$44K/year price gap (Dietrich $325,248 vs Foreman $280,896 for 176 school days). Mitchell entity's status post-loss is unclear; parent Dietrich & Sons remains active in Valley City, ND with bus sales and other contracts.
fit_notes: Family-run multi-generational operator; Mitchell SD entity lost flagship district 2017 so the SD location may be dormant or de-scaled. Parent in ND retains bus operations and dealership — broader Dietrich family enterprise could be a larger conversation than just the SD subsidiary, but not a clear independent tuck-in in SD.

name: Mobility Plus Transportation
districts_or_customers: Contra Costa County NEMT/paratransit clientele (private pay and likely Medi-Cal/regional center)
recent_news: No M&A news identified (2024-2026). Operates as a Bay Area NEMT/wheelchair-van provider; listed on Yellow Pages and directories serving Concord and broader Contra Costa County.
fit_notes: NEMT/paratransit van operator rather than yellow-bus or SPED route contractor. Tagged 'special_needs' in source but the business model is medical/ADA wheelchair transport, not K-12 SPED routing. Marginal fit for Summit unless platform pursues SPED/NEMT-adjacent rollups in CA.

name: Art Of Care
districts_or_customers: MassHealth / Home Care Alliance of Massachusetts member; adult foster care recipients in Greater Boston
recent_news: Affiliated Brighton entity Absolute Care Inc. (also tied to Boris Yablonovsky) paid $3 million to MA AG (announced by Mass.gov) to resolve allegations of false MassHealth adult foster care billing since 2013 via falsified caregiver timesheets — material reputational/regulatory flag for the Yablonovsky cluster of companies.
fit_notes: Primarily an adult foster care / home care agency, not a school-bus or K-12 SPED route contractor. Affiliated entity's $3M Medicaid fraud settlement is a hard exclusion criterion for an I Squared / Summit platform acquisition. Recommend remove from target list.
